uses: Cyclosorus malodorus, also known as the stinking fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. It is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties. The plant's roots and rhizomes are also used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions like eczema. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat digestive issues and as a diuretic. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Cyclosorus malodorus for medicinal purposes.
